title: PD-EEG: Resting-State & Walking EEG in Parkinson's Disease
description: This dataset comprises resting-state and walking EEG recordings from 116 Parkinson's disease patients and 28 healthy controls, acquired using a 64-channel Geodesic EEG System. Each participant underwent approximately 4-minute EEG sessions during seated rest (eyes open) and treadmill walking at comfortable speed. The dataset includes comprehensive clinical assessments (MDS-UPDRS, MoCA, LEDD, CTT) and is organized according to BIDS standards, enabling investigation of neurophysiological differences between disease and control groups across behavioral states.
methods_description: EEG was recorded using a 64-channel Geodesic EEG System 400 (EGI system) with International 10–20 montage. Two behavioral conditions were assessed: resting state (sitting, eyes open, ~4 minutes) and walking (treadmill at comfortable speed with handrail support, ~4 minutes). Clinical data included demographic information, LEDD, MoCA, MDS-UPDRS, and CTT assessments.
